voice_transcript: Hi, I dropped my BlenderMax 3000 yesterday and now it's making a grinding noise whenever I turn it on. The blade assembly looks bent.
image_description: Close-up of blender base showing visible crack on housing and bent blade assembly
text_context: Product: BlenderMax 3000. SKU: BM3K-2024. Warranty: 2 years from purchase. Coverage: Manufacturing defects only. Exclusions: Physical damage from drops, misuse. Return window: 30 days. Replacement parts available: blade assembly ($29), motor unit ($59).
ground_truth_reason: Physical damage from drop is excluded from warranty. Voice confirms drop, image confirms physical damage. Recommend offering replacement blade assembly at customer cost.

voice_transcript: I just unboxed this coffee maker and the carafe has a crack in it right out of the box. I haven't even used it yet.
image_description: New coffee maker carafe with hairline crack visible near handle, product still in original packaging
text_context: Product: BrewPerfect Pro. SKU: BPP-500. Warranty: 1 year. Coverage: Manufacturing defects and damage on arrival. Return window: 30 days for full refund. Free replacement for DOA items within 7 days.
ground_truth_reason: Damage on arrival is covered. Voice confirms unboxing, image confirms crack, product still in packaging. Within DOA replacement window.

voice_transcript: My toaster has been sparking inside when I use it. I've only had it for two weeks. I'm scared to use it now.
image_description: Interior of toaster showing blackened heating element with visible burn marks on crumb tray
text_context: Product: CrispMaster Toaster 4-Slice. SKU: CMT-400. Warranty: 1 year. Coverage: Electrical defects, heating element failure. Safety policy: Any sparking or fire hazard reports are escalated immediately. Recall status: None active.
ground_truth_reason: Sparking is a safety hazard. Image confirms electrical damage. Must immediately replace and flag for quality review. Customer safety is priority.

voice_transcript: I bought this laptop stand three months ago and one of the hinges just snapped off while I was adjusting it normally.
image_description: Aluminum laptop stand with broken hinge, clean break at hinge joint suggesting material fatigue
text_context: Product: ErgoLift Pro Stand. SKU: ELP-200. Warranty: 3 years. Coverage: Structural defects, hinge mechanism. Weight limit: 20 lbs. Customer purchase history: Laptop weighs 4.5 lbs (within spec).
ground_truth_reason: Hinge failure within warranty, customer using within weight spec. Clean break suggests manufacturing defect. Approve replacement under structural defects coverage.

voice_transcript: My kid used this tablet for a month and the screen started flickering. Then yesterday the whole display went black but you can still hear sounds.
image_description: Tablet with black screen, no visible physical damage, power LED is green indicating device is on
text_context: Product: LearnTab Kids Tablet. SKU: LTK-8. Warranty: 2 years. Coverage: Display failure, battery issues, software defects. Exclusions: Water damage, drop damage (check moisture indicator). Return window: 30 days for refund, then warranty repair/replace.
ground_truth_reason: Display failure with no visible physical damage. Image shows device powers on (LED green) but screen black. Classic display connector or panel failure. Covered under warranty.

voice_transcript: I've been using this pan for about eight months and the non-stick coating is peeling off into my food. This can't be safe.
image_description: Non-stick frying pan with visible coating deterioration, several patches where coating has peeled away exposing metal underneath
text_context: Product: ChefLine Pro Non-Stick Pan. SKU: CLP-12. Warranty: Lifetime on construction, 2 years on coating. Coverage: Coating degradation under normal use. Exclusions: Metal utensil damage, dishwasher use, overheating above 500°F. Care instructions: Hand wash only, use plastic or wooden utensils.
ground_truth_reason: Coating peeling after 8 months could be defect or misuse. Need specialist to review usage patterns and image to determine if dishwasher or metal utensil damage vs manufacturing defect. Food safety concern requires careful review.

voice_transcript: My wireless earbuds won't charge anymore. I've tried different cables and outlets but the case LED doesn't light up at all.
image_description: Wireless earbuds charging case with charging port visible, no visible damage, connected to USB cable with no LED response
text_context: Product: SoundPods Pro. SKU: SPP-22. Warranty: 1 year. Coverage: Battery and charging defects. Troubleshooting: 1. Try different cable. 2. Clean charging port. 3. Press reset button for 10s. If LED still dead, likely charging circuit failure.
ground_truth_reason: Customer tried different cables. Image shows clean port and proper connection. No LED response indicates charging circuit failure, which is covered under battery/charging defects warranty.

voice_transcript: The stitching on my backpack shoulder strap came undone after just two weeks. I only use it for my laptop and lunch.
image_description: Backpack shoulder strap showing unraveled stitching at the attachment point, thread visibly loose
text_context: Product: TrekPro Commuter Pack. SKU: TPC-30. Warranty: Lifetime on stitching and zippers. Coverage: All stitching defects regardless of use duration. Max load: 35 lbs. Weight class: Suitable for laptops up to 17 inches.
ground_truth_reason: Lifetime stitching warranty covers this defect. Image clearly shows stitching failure. Customer use (laptop + lunch) is well within designed capacity. Immediate replacement authorized.

voice_transcript: I got this robot vacuum last week and it keeps bumping into everything even though the sensors should stop it. It's scratched my furniture.
image_description: Robot vacuum with cloudy/scratched sensor dome on front, likely obscuring IR sensors
text_context: Product: CleanBot X1. SKU: CBX1-24. Warranty: 2 years. Coverage: Motor, battery, sensor defects. Sensor types: IR proximity (front dome), cliff sensors (bottom). Common issues: Cloudy sensor dome = defect, dirty sensors = user maintenance. Troubleshooting: Clean sensors with microfiber cloth. If cloudy from inside, manufacturing defect.
ground_truth_reason: Image shows cloudy sensor dome (internal issue, not dirt). This is a manufacturing defect preventing proper navigation. Within warranty, covered under sensor defects.

voice_transcript: My pressure cooker lid won't seal properly anymore. Steam keeps escaping from the sides even when the valve is closed.
image_description: Pressure cooker lid gasket showing visible deformation and gaps, rubber appears warped
text_context: Product: InstaCook Pro 8Qt. SKU: ICP-8. Warranty: 2 years on unit, gasket is consumable (6-month coverage). Coverage: Lid mechanism, heating element, electronics. Consumables: Gasket ($12), steam valve ($8). Customer purchase: 4 months ago.
ground_truth_reason: Gasket failure at 4 months is within 6-month consumable coverage. Image confirms warped gasket. Order free replacement gasket under warranty. Not a unit replacement issue.

voice_transcript: I dropped my phone in the toilet briefly and now the screen has water under it. I know I shouldn't have but is there anything you can do?
image_description: Smartphone screen showing visible water spots trapped under the display glass
text_context: Product: TechPhone X12. SKU: TPX-12. Warranty: 1 year. Coverage: Manufacturing defects. Exclusions: Water damage (check LDI - Liquid Damage Indicator inside SIM tray). Water resistance: IP67 rated for 30 min at 1m depth. Note: Toilet water may contain chemicals that void water resistance claims.
ground_truth_reason: Customer admits water submersion. Water damage is explicitly excluded. IP67 rating is not a guarantee against all water exposure. Recommend professional repair service at customer cost.

voice_transcript: My electric kettle started making a burning smell yesterday. I unplugged it immediately. The water heats fine but the smell is concerning.
image_description: Electric kettle base showing slight discoloration around heating element connection point
text_context: Product: RapidBoil Kettle 1.7L. SKU: RBK-17. Warranty: 2 years. Coverage: Electrical defects, heating element. Safety protocol: Any burning smell = immediate stop use + expedited replacement. No repair attempts - full replacement only for electrical smell reports.
ground_truth_reason: Burning smell is safety hazard indicating electrical issue. Image shows discoloration at heating connection. Per safety protocol, immediate replacement with no repair attempt. Flag for quality review.

voice_transcript: I'm on step 4 where it says to attach the crossbar but I can't figure out which screw to use. There are three different sizes in the bag.
text_context:
IKEA KALLAX Shelf Assembly Instructions:
Step 1: Lay side panels flat.
Step 2: Insert wooden dowels into pre-drilled holes.
Step 3: Attach top panel using cam locks (rotate 90° clockwise).
Step 4: Attach crossbar dividers using 35mm Phillips screws (medium size, silver). The small screws (20mm) are for the back panel in Step 6. The large screws (50mm) are for wall mounting in Step 8.
Step 5: Repeat for remaining crossbars.
ground_truth_reason: User is on Step 4. The correct screw is the 35mm medium silver Phillips screw. Voice identifies the confusion point, text context provides the specific answer.

voice_transcript: I'm trying to put together this office chair and I'm stuck on attaching the armrests. The manual shows arrows but I can't tell which way they're supposed to go.
text_context:
ErgoTask Office Chair Assembly:
Armrest Installation (Step 7):
- Left armrest has 'L' marking on underside, right has 'R'
- Curved edge faces FORWARD (toward front of seat)
- Insert bolts from underneath seat, armrest bracket goes on top
- Tighten with included Allen key (4mm), do not overtighten
- Armrests should click into adjustment slots when properly seated
ground_truth_reason: User confused about armrest orientation. Key info: L/R markings on underside, curved edge faces forward. Bolts insert from underneath.

voice_transcript: Hello, I'm assembling a baby crib and I'm really nervous about getting it right. The mattress support has multiple height settings and I don't know which one to start with.
text_context:
SafeSleep Crib Assembly - Mattress Support Heights:
- Position 1 (Highest): For newborns 0-5 months or until baby can push up on hands and knees
- Position 2 (Middle): For babies 5-8 months who can sit but not pull to stand
- Position 3 (Lowest): REQUIRED once baby can pull to standing position
SAFETY: Always start at highest position for newborns. Lower immediately if baby shows signs of pulling up. Gap between mattress and crib sides must be less than 2 fingers width.
ground_truth_reason: For a new baby, start at Position 1 (highest). Voice conveys concern about safety - reassure about starting position and provide the 2-finger gap safety check.

voice_transcript: I'm assembling this TV stand and I have these weird metal pieces left over. They look like brackets but the instructions don't mention them anywhere.
image_description: Two L-shaped metal anti-tip brackets with pre-drilled holes, alongside wall anchors and screws
text_context:
MediaCenter TV Stand Assembly - Anti-Tip Kit:
- Anti-tip brackets are REQUIRED for safety (prevents furniture tip-over)
- NOT shown in main assembly steps - see separate Safety Sheet in box
- Installation: Attach one bracket to back of stand, one to wall stud
- Required for stands holding TVs over 40 inches or in homes with children
- Wall anchors provided for drywall installation if stud not available
ground_truth_reason: Image shows anti-tip brackets which are safety-required but on separate sheet. Direct user to Safety Sheet, explain these mount stand to wall for tip-over prevention.

voice_transcript: I finished assembling my desk but it's really wobbly. All the screws are tight. What did I do wrong?
text_context:
SturdyDesk Pro Troubleshooting - Wobble Issues:
1. Check all 8 leg bolts are fully tightened (common: bottom bolts forgotten)
2. Verify crossbar is installed (provides lateral stability) - Step 5
3. Ensure all 4 feet pads are level - adjust threaded feet as needed
4. Check surface is level - use leveling feet to compensate for uneven floors
5. Frame alignment: If assembled on carpet, lift desk and set down on hard floor to reset alignment
Most common cause: Missing crossbar installation from Step 5.
ground_truth_reason: Wobble with tight screws usually means missing crossbar or uneven feet. Guide user through checklist: verify crossbar is installed, then check feet leveling.

voice_transcript: The cam lock on my bookshelf won't turn. I've inserted the wooden dowel and the screw but the cam just spins without tightening anything.
text_context:
Cam Lock Troubleshooting:
- Cam must engage with cam bolt head, not just spin freely
- Common error: Cam bolt not fully inserted - must thread completely into adjoining panel
- Check: Cam bolt head should protrude exactly 10mm into cam hole
- If cam spins: Back out cam bolt, verify threads engaged with panel, re-insert fully
- Arrow on cam should point toward panel edge when starting, rotate 90-180° to lock
- Do not force - cross-threading will strip the particle board
ground_truth_reason: Spinning cam indicates cam bolt not fully engaged. User needs to back out bolt, ensure threads engaged with panel, reinsert so head protrudes 10mm, then turn cam.

voice_transcript: I'm putting together a bunk bed for my kids. The instructions say to use the support slats but I have more slats than spaces. Do I have extras?
text_context:
DreamLoft Bunk Bed - Slat Configuration:
- Upper bunk: 12 slats required, spaced evenly across frame
- Lower bunk: 14 slats required (additional support for lower mattress)
- Total slats provided: 26 (no extras)
- Slats are interchangeable between beds but ALL must be used
- Maximum gap between slats: 3 inches (safety requirement)
- Center support beam must be installed before slats on lower bunk
ground_truth_reason: No extras - 12 for top, 14 for bottom, total 26. User may have miscounted spaces or not realized lower bunk needs more. Clarify distribution and safety spacing requirement.

voice_transcript: I'm installing a ceiling fan and I can't figure out which wires connect to which. There are four wires coming out of the ceiling.
image_description: Electrical junction box showing four wires: black, white, bare copper, and red wire
text_context:
SAFETY WARNING: Turn off power at circuit breaker before any wiring work.

Standard Ceiling Fan Wiring (4-wire setup with separate light control):
- Black (ceiling) to Black (fan) = Fan power
- Red (ceiling) to Blue (fan) = Light power (allows separate switch control)
- White (ceiling) to White (fan) = Neutral
- Bare copper = Ground, attach to green fan wire and junction box ground screw

If no separate light switch desired: Cap red wire with wire nut and connect fan's blue to black.
ground_truth_reason: Image shows 4-wire setup (black, white, copper, red). Provide color matching: black-black for fan, red-blue for light, white-white neutral, copper to ground. Emphasize safety: power must be OFF.

voice_transcript: My treadmill display panel isn't turning on after I assembled it. The motor seems to work when I tested it.
text_context:
FitPro Treadmill - Display Panel Troubleshooting:
- Display connects via two cables: data ribbon (thin flat cable) and power connector (4-pin Molex)
- Both must be fully seated - listen for click on Molex connector
- Ribbon cable has correct orientation: blue side faces UP toward display
- Common issue: Ribbon cable inserted upside down (works intermittently or not at all)
- Test: If motor works but display dead, power is reaching base - issue is display connection
- Do not force connectors - misalignment will bend pins
ground_truth_reason: Motor works means power is good. Display issue is likely connection problem. Guide user to check ribbon cable orientation (blue side up) and ensure Molex connector clicked fully.

voice_transcript: I'm trying to assemble my gas grill and I'm nervous about connecting the propane tank. How do I know it's on correctly and not leaking?
text_context:
GrillMaster Pro - Propane Connection Safety:
1. Ensure all burner knobs are OFF position before connecting tank
2. Tank valve must be fully closed (clockwise until stops)
3. Hand-tighten regulator to tank valve (left-hand thread - turn counterclockwise to tighten)
4. Open tank valve slowly (one full turn)
5. LEAK TEST: Apply soapy water to connection. Bubbles = leak. Tighten or replace regulator.
6. If no bubbles, connection is safe. Open valve fully.
NEVER use open flame to test for leaks. If persistent leak, replace regulator.
ground_truth_reason: Safety-critical assembly. Walk through: knobs off, valve closed, regulator on (left-hand thread!), slow open, soapy water test. Emphasize: never use flame, bubbles mean leak.

voice_transcript: The sliding doors on my new wardrobe keep jumping off the track. I've reinstalled them twice and they still pop out.
text_context:
GlideRobe Wardrobe - Sliding Door Troubleshooting:
- Doors use top-hanging system with bottom guide
- Installation order: Insert top wheels into track FIRST, then swing bottom into guide
- Top track adjustment: Screws at track ends adjust track height (lower = tighter fit)
- Bottom guide: Must be centered under door - misalignment causes derailing
- Wheel adjustment: Each door has adjustment screws on top to level the door
- Common issue: Bottom guide installed before top track leveled - recalibrate from top down
ground_truth_reason: Doors jumping off usually means track height wrong or bottom guide misaligned. Instruct: adjust top track screws to lower track slightly for tighter wheel fit, then center bottom guide.

voice_transcript: I assembled the bed frame but the wooden slats keep sliding around when I move. Is there something to hold them in place?
text_context:
SleepWell Bed Frame - Slat Securing Options:
- Slats should rest in notched ledges on frame sides
- If ledges are smooth (no notches), use slat holders: small plastic clips that snap onto frame rail
- Check hardware bag for slat holder clips (bag labeled 'S')
- Alternative: Fabric slat holder (connects all slats with canvas strip) - separate purchase if not included
- For slatted bunkie boards: These are pre-connected and don't slide
- Do not glue or permanently attach slats - ventilation gaps are intentional for mattress airflow
ground_truth_reason: Direct user to check hardware bag 'S' for slat holder clips. If not included, fabric slat holder is an accessory purchase. Explain slats intentionally have gaps for airflow.

image_description: Router admin page showing WAN status: disconnected, LAN status: active, DNS: 0.0.0.0
text_context:
Troubleshooting Tree - Router No Internet:
1. Check WAN status → If 'disconnected': Check physical cable connection to modem. If cable is secure, power cycle modem (unplug 30s).
2. Check DNS → If '0.0.0.0': ISP DNS not resolving. Try setting manual DNS to 8.8.8.8 and 8.8.4.4.
3. Check LAN → If 'active' but no internet: Issue is upstream (WAN/ISP side), not local network.
4. If all above fail: Escalate to ISP support.
ground_truth_reason: Image shows WAN disconnected + DNS 0.0.0.0 + LAN active. Per troubleshooting tree: first check physical WAN cable, then power cycle modem. LAN active confirms local network is fine — issue is upstream.

image_description: Washing machine error display showing code 'E3' and 'dL' indicator lit
text_context:
CleanWash Pro Error Codes:
- E1: Water inlet problem (check hoses, water supply)
- E2: Drain problem (check drain hose, filter)
- E3: Door lock failure (dL indicator) - door not properly secured or lock mechanism stuck
- E4: Unbalanced load (redistribute clothes)
- E5: Motor overload (reduce load size)
E3 Resolution: 1. Power off, wait 2 min. 2. Check door seal for obstructions. 3. Close door firmly until click. 4. If persists, door lock actuator may need replacement.
ground_truth_reason: Image shows E3 + dL lit = door lock failure. Guide: power cycle, check door seal for stuck items, close firmly. If error persists, door lock actuator needs service.

image_description: 3D printer with filament wound around extruder gear, visible tangle near feeder mechanism
text_context:
FilaPrint 3D Printer Troubleshooting - Filament Jams:
- Tangle at feeder: Filament kinked on spool or feeder tension too tight
- Fix: Cut filament above tangle, manually unwind, reduce tension knob by 1/4 turn
- Prevention: Use spool holder that allows free rotation, store filament dry
- Extruder wrapped: COLD pull method - heat to 200°C, then cool to 90°C and pull firmly
- If gear teeth clogged: Clean with brass brush, do not use steel (damages gear)
WARNING: Never pull filament when extruder is at print temperature - risks internal break
ground_truth_reason: Image shows tangle at extruder gear. User should: cut filament above tangle, manually remove wound filament, reduce feeder tension. Clean gear teeth with brass brush if needed.

image_description: HVAC thermostat display showing 'Heat On' but room temperature 58°F with setpoint 72°F, 45 minutes elapsed
text_context:
SmartTemp Thermostat - Heat Not Reaching Setpoint:
- If 'Heat On' shows but temp not rising after 15+ min:
1. Check air filter - clogged filter restricts airflow (most common cause)
2. Verify all vents are open and unobstructed
3. Check furnace - go to unit, verify it's actually running (fan noise, warm air from plenum)
4. If furnace running but no heat: Pilot light out (gas) or heating element issue (electric)
5. If furnace not running: Check furnace power switch, circuit breaker
6. Thermostat wire issue: Rare, but check Y/W wire connection at thermostat backplate
ground_truth_reason: Image shows 14°F gap after 45 min - system not heating despite 'Heat On'. Start with most common: check air filter, verify vents open, then check if furnace is actually running.

voice_transcript: My car won't start. When I turn the key I hear clicking but nothing else happens. The headlights seem dim too.
image_description: Car dashboard showing battery warning light illuminated, dim lighting overall
text_context:
Vehicle No-Start Diagnosis - Clicking Sound:
- Rapid clicking + dim lights = Battery too weak to engage starter motor
- Single click = Starter motor failure (different issue, needs mechanic)
Battery weak causes:
1. Battery age (typical life 3-5 years)
2. Alternator not charging (battery light was on while driving)
3. Parasitic drain (light left on, faulty module)
Immediate solution: Jump start
- Connect red to positive (+), black to negative (-) or ground point
- Let donor car run 5 min before attempting start
After starting: Drive 20+ min to recharge, or battery may need replacement
ground_truth_reason: Clicking + dim lights + battery light = dead/weak battery. Voice confirms no-start, image confirms battery issue. Provide jump start instructions, advise battery test after.

image_description: Laptop keyboard with several keys showing ghosting - pressing one key triggers multiple letters
text_context:
Laptop Keyboard Troubleshooting - Ghost Typing/Multi-key Press:
- Ghosting (multiple keys per press) causes:
1. Liquid damage - even dried spills cause conductive residue
2. Dust/debris under keys - causes false contacts
3. Failing keyboard membrane - age/wear issue
4. Driver issue - rare but possible
Diagnosis: External USB keyboard works fine = internal keyboard hardware issue
Solutions:
- Debris: Compressed air, keyboard upside-down shake
- Liquid: Professional cleaning or keyboard replacement
- Driver: Device Manager > Keyboards > Uninstall > Reboot
If hardware: Laptop keyboard replacement usually $50-100 + labor
ground_truth_reason: Image shows ghosting behavior. Recommend: test with external USB keyboard to confirm internal issue. Try compressed air for debris. If liquid damage suspected or persists, keyboard replacement needed.

image_description: Smart TV screen showing horizontal lines across display, lines appear at regular intervals
text_context:
SmartView TV Display Issues:
- Horizontal lines (regular intervals): Usually T-Con board or ribbon cable issue
- Vertical lines: Panel damage, typically not repairable cost-effectively
- Random color blocks: GPU/main board failure
T-Con board troubleshooting:
1. Power off and unplug TV for 5 minutes
2. Check ribbon cables from T-Con to panel - reseat firmly
3. If lines persist: T-Con board replacement ($40-80 part, intermediate DIY)
4. If model <3 years old and no physical damage: Check warranty
Panel replacement cost often exceeds TV value - consider replacement if panel is the issue.
ground_truth_reason: Image shows horizontal lines at regular intervals - likely T-Con board or ribbon cable. Guide: unplug 5 min, reseat ribbon cables. If persists, T-Con board replacement is moderate DIY fix.

image_description: Dishwasher interior showing white film/residue on all dishes and interior walls
text_context:
AquaClean Dishwasher - White Film/Residue:
Causes:
1. Hard water mineral deposits (most common) - calcium/lime buildup
2. Too much detergent - causes residue, especially with soft water
3. Water not hot enough - detergent not dissolving fully
4. Rinse aid empty or dispenser clogged
Solutions:
- Hard water: Run empty cycle with 2 cups white vinegar in top rack
- Add rinse aid and ensure dispenser is set to max for hard water
- Check water heater temp (120-140°F optimal)
- Reduce detergent if water is soft (check with test strip)
- Dishwasher cleaner monthly in hard water areas
ground_truth_reason: Image shows widespread white film - classic hard water deposits. Recommend vinegar cycle, check/refill rinse aid at max setting, verify water temperature 120-140°F.

image_description: Desktop PC with power LED on but no display output, monitor shows 'No Signal' message
text_context:
PC Powers On But No Display:
1. Check monitor input source - must match cable type (HDMI, DP, etc.)
2. Reseat video cable at both ends
3. If discrete GPU: Ensure monitor plugged into GPU, not motherboard
4. RAM issue: Most common - open case, reseat RAM sticks firmly
5. GPU issue: Remove and reseat graphics card, check power connectors
6. BIOS reset: Remove CMOS battery 5 min, replace
7. Listen for beep codes: Different patterns indicate different failures
Diagnostic: If onboard video works (GPU removed), issue is GPU or PCIe slot.
ground_truth_reason: Image shows power but no signal. Key steps: verify monitor input matches cable, confirm cable in GPU not motherboard. If issue persists, open case and reseat RAM - most common cause.

image_description: Refrigerator interior showing frost buildup on back wall of freezer compartment, ice forming on vents
text_context:
CoolZone Refrigerator - Frost Buildup Diagnosis:
- Frost on back wall/vents = Defrost system failure
- Components: Defrost heater, defrost thermostat, defrost timer (or control board)
Diagnosis:
1. Defrost heater: Should feel warm during defrost cycle
2. Defrost thermostat: Should have continuity below 37°F
3. Timer/board: Controls defrost cycle every 8-10 hours
Immediate fix: Manual defrost - unplug 24 hours with doors open
Long-term: Usually defrost heater failure ($20-50 part)
Note: Some frost is normal, excessive buildup affecting airflow is the problem.
ground_truth_reason: Image shows frost on vents - defrost system failure. Immediate: manual defrost (unplug 24hr). Long-term likely defrost heater replacement. If ice blocks vents, cooling is compromised.

voice_transcript: My garage door goes down about a foot and then immediately reverses back up. The sensors seem fine, both lights are on.
image_description: Garage door opener showing force adjustment dials, and photo eye sensors with both LEDs lit green
text_context:
GaragePro Opener - Door Reverses on Close:
If both sensor LEDs are green (aligned), reversal is not sensor-triggered.
Other causes:
1. Close force too low - Increase close force dial (small increments)
2. Track obstruction or binding - Inspect tracks for debris, bent sections
3. Spring tension incorrect - Door should lift easily with opener disconnected
4. Roller damage - Worn rollers cause friction
5. Safety bracket triggered - Close limit set too far
Adjustment: Close force dial - turn clockwise 1/4 turn, test. Repeat until door closes fully.
Note: Door should reverse if it hits true obstruction - test with 2x4 on floor.
ground_truth_reason: Voice says reverses after 1 foot, image confirms sensors aligned (green). Issue is likely close force set too low. Guide: increase close force dial 1/4 turn clockwise, test, repeat until closes.

image_description: Espresso machine portafilter showing uneven extraction - one side with brown crema, other side pale/watery
text_context:
BaristaPro Espresso - Uneven Extraction (Channeling):
Image symptom: Uneven color in portafilter = water finding path of least resistance
Causes:
1. Uneven tamping - tamp must be level with consistent 30lb pressure
2. Uneven distribution - grounds piled to one side before tamping
3. Worn group head gasket - causes side spray
4. Cracked basket - water bypasses grounds
Fix:
- Distribution: Use WDT tool or tap basket sides before tamping
- Tamping: Use calibrated tamper, practice level technique
- Clean group head: Backflush with cleaner, inspect gasket
- If basket cracked: Replace ($8-15)
ground_truth_reason: Image shows channeling - uneven extraction. User needs to improve grounds distribution (WDT tool or tapping) and ensure level tamp with consistent pressure. Also check group head gasket condition.

voice_transcript: I'm really frustrated. I bought this vacuum six months ago and the suction has completely died. I've cleaned the filter, replaced the bag, everything. It just doesn't work anymore. I need this fixed or replaced ASAP, I have guests coming this weekend.
image_description: Vacuum cleaner model label showing serial number VC-2024-88431, manufacture date 2024-09, product appears externally undamaged
text_context: Product: TurboClean V8 Vacuum. Warranty: 2 years parts and labor. Coverage: Motor failure, suction loss (if filter maintenance documented), electrical defects. Process: Warranty claims require serial number verification + proof of purchase. Expedited replacement available for confirmed motor failure (ships within 2 business days).
ground_truth_reason: Suction loss after 6 months with documented filter maintenance attempts is covered. Voice conveys urgency (guests this weekend) — recommend expedited replacement. Image provides serial number for verification. Within 2-year warranty period.

voice_transcript: Hi, I bought these headphones last month and the left ear cup just stopped working. The right side is fine but I can't hear anything from the left.
image_description: Over-ear headphones showing no visible damage, product looks new, serial number visible on headband
text_context: Product: AudioMax Elite Headphones. SKU: AME-400. Warranty: 2 years. Coverage: Driver failure, connectivity issues, structural defects. Common issues: Left/right imbalance (driver failure), Bluetooth cutting out (firmware), broken hinges (covered). Verification: Serial number + purchase date.
ground_truth_reason: One-sided audio failure at 1 month is driver failure, covered under warranty. Image shows no damage, serial visible. Straightforward warranty replacement.

voice_transcript: My air fryer stopped heating about two weeks after the warranty ended. I paid good money for this and it should last longer than 13 months.
image_description: Air fryer exterior in good condition, display shows power on but temperature reading stuck at room temp
text_context: Product: CrispAir Pro 6Qt. SKU: CAP-6. Warranty: 1 year from purchase date. Post-warranty options: Goodwill extension (manager approval, case-by-case for failures within 60 days of expiry), Paid repair service ($45 flat rate), Discount on replacement (15% loyalty offer).
ground_truth_reason: Warranty expired by 2 weeks. Per policy, within 60-day goodwill window. Escalate to manager for goodwill extension approval. Customer kept product in good condition (per image), reasonable request.

voice_transcript: I need to file a warranty claim. My laptop battery swelled up and pushed the trackpad out of the case. I'm worried it might explode.
image_description: Laptop showing bulging bottom case, trackpad visibly raised above palm rest surface, gap visible between case halves
text_context: Product: WorkBook Pro 15. Warranty: 3 years on components, battery has separate 1 year (consumable). SAFETY POLICY: Swollen battery is FIRE HAZARD regardless of warranty status. Immediate response required: Do not charge or use device, store away from flammables, expedite replacement at no cost (safety exception).
ground_truth_reason: Swollen battery is safety hazard - triggers safety exception policy. Replace entire unit immediately regardless of warranty status on battery. Instruct customer to stop use immediately and safe storage.

voice_transcript: The glass door on my microwave cracked while I was just standing there. I didn't do anything to it. It just suddenly cracked.
image_description: Microwave door with spiderweb crack pattern originating from corner, no impact point visible
text_context: Product: QuickHeat Pro Microwave. Warranty: 1 year. Coverage: Electrical, magnetron, control panel. Glass door: Covered if manufacturing defect (stress crack from tempered glass failure). Not covered: Impact damage (identifiable by impact point and radial cracks from point). Stress cracks: Originate from edges/corners, no impact point visible.
ground_truth_reason: Crack pattern from corner with no impact point = tempered glass stress failure (manufacturing defect). Image confirms spiderweb pattern from corner, not impact. Covered under warranty.

voice_transcript: My refrigerator is making a loud grinding noise from the back. It's been going on for a week and getting worse. We bought it about 18 months ago.
image_description: Refrigerator compressor area showing frost buildup around fan blade, fan blade appears to be contacting ice
text_context: Product: FreshKeep French Door Refrigerator. Warranty: 2 years parts and labor, 5 years on sealed system (compressor). Grinding from rear: Usually evaporator fan hitting ice buildup. Causes: Defrost system failure allowing ice accumulation. Fan blade or motor replacement: Covered part. Service call may be required.
ground_truth_reason: Grinding noise from fan hitting ice at 18 months, within 2-year warranty. Image confirms ice buildup at fan. Defrost system issue causing ice - covered under warranty. Schedule service call.

voice_transcript: I bought this smart watch for running and it's only been three months but the heart rate sensor is giving completely wrong readings. It says my heart rate is 40 when I'm running full speed.
image_description: Smartwatch on wrist showing heart rate display of 42 BPM, person's wrist appears sweaty indicating exercise
text_context: Product: FitTrack Elite Watch. Warranty: 2 years. Coverage: Sensor accuracy, battery, display, bands. Known issues: HR sensor inaccurate if worn too loose, if sensor dirty, or if sensor hardware failure. Troubleshooting: Clean sensor with damp cloth, wear snug (2-finger width from wrist bone), check for firmware update. If persists after troubleshooting: Sensor replacement under warranty.
ground_truth_reason: HR reading 42 during running is clearly wrong. Image shows proper wear position. At 3 months, if basic troubleshooting (cleaning, fit adjustment) doesn't resolve, this is sensor hardware failure - covered under warranty.

voice_transcript: My power drill's chuck won't tighten anymore. The jaws don't grip the bit properly no matter how much I tighten it. I use it for work so I need it fixed fast.
image_description: Cordless drill with chuck visibly worn, jaws don't close evenly, one jaw sitting higher than others
text_context: Product: ProDrill 20V Max. Warranty: 3 years for professional users (registration required), 5 years for consumer use. Chuck: Considered wear item after 2 years of heavy use. Coverage first 2 years: Any failure. Years 2-5: Manufacturing defects only, not wear. Customer registration: Professional, registered 11 months ago.
ground_truth_reason: Professional registration at 11 months ago, within first 2 years where any chuck failure is covered. Image shows uneven jaw wear/failure. Approve warranty replacement or repair.

voice_transcript: I've had this gaming monitor for eight months and now there's a dead pixel cluster right in the center of the screen. It's super annoying during games.
image_description: Monitor displaying white background with visible cluster of 5 dead pixels (black dots) near center of screen
text_context: Product: GameView 27 144Hz Monitor. Warranty: 3 years. Pixel Policy: 0 dead pixel guarantee in center region (inner 80% of screen). Outside center: 5+ dead pixels for warranty. Bright pixels (stuck on): 1 or more in center = warranty. Dead pixel cluster (adjacent pixels): Always covered regardless of location. Image verification required for pixel claims.
ground_truth_reason: Cluster of 5 adjacent dead pixels in center is covered under both cluster policy (always covered) and center region policy (0 pixel guarantee). Image clearly shows cluster in center. Approve replacement.

voice_transcript: My portable AC unit is leaking water inside my apartment. There's water all over my floor. I've only had it two months and used it normally.
image_description: Portable AC unit with visible water pooling at base, drain plug visible and appears properly closed
text_context: Product: CoolBreeze 12000 BTU Portable AC. Warranty: 2 years. Coverage: Compressor, fan, electrical. Common issues: Water leak from internal tank = condensate drain clogged or pump failure. Condensate pump: Covered component. User maintenance: Empty tank when full indicator lights (not required on self-evaporative models). This model: Fully self-evaporative, should not require manual draining.
ground_truth_reason: Self-evaporative unit shouldn't leak - condensate pump failure or internal clog (covered components). Image shows proper drain plug position. At 2 months, clear manufacturing/component defect. Approve warranty service.

voice_transcript: I accidentally washed my wireless earbuds in the laundry. Is there any chance they're covered under warranty? I know it's my fault but they were expensive.
image_description: Wireless earbuds showing water residue, charging case slightly deformed from water/heat exposure
text_context: Product: SoundPods Ultra. Warranty: 1 year. Coverage: Battery, driver, charging defects. Exclusions: Water damage, heat damage, physical abuse. Water resistance: IPX4 (splash resistant, NOT submersion). Accidental damage protection: Available as separate purchase ($49), covers drops and liquid damage. Customer has standard warranty only.
ground_truth_reason: Customer admits laundry washing = clear water damage beyond IPX4 rating. Standard warranty explicitly excludes water damage. Image confirms water and heat damage (case deformation). Recommend ADP for future purchases.

voice_transcript: My robot mop keeps stopping and showing an error code. I clean the sensors regularly but it just won't work properly. This is the third time this month.
image_description: Robot mop display showing error code 'E14', cliff sensors appear clean, overall device looks maintained
text_context: Product: MopBot Pro. Warranty: 2 years. Error E14: Navigation sensor calibration failure. Causes: Hardware defect in laser distance sensor (LDS), or main board communication error. User-serviceable: No. E14 resolution requires service center or replacement. If E14 recurs after reset 3+ times: Hardware defect confirmed, warranty replacement authorized.
ground_truth_reason: E14 error recurring 3+ times confirms hardware defect per troubleshooting tree. Customer maintains device well (image shows clean sensors). Authorize warranty replacement.

voice_transcript: The zipper on my carry-on suitcase broke during my flight last week. I was pulling it normally and it just separated from the fabric.
image_description: Carry-on suitcase showing zipper separated from fabric track, stitching intact suggesting zipper tape failure not torn fabric
text_context: Product: TravelPro Carry-On 22. Warranty: Lifetime on zippers and wheels. Coverage: All zipper failures, wheel/bearing failures, handle mechanism. Airline damage: File claim with airline first, we cover if airline denies. Zipper separation from tape: Manufacturing defect (different from fabric tear). Stitching intact = not user abuse.
ground_truth_reason: Lifetime zipper warranty, image shows zipper separated from tape with stitching intact = manufacturing defect, not fabric tear or abuse. Approve warranty repair or replacement.

voice_transcript: I got this air purifier a few weeks ago and it's making a high-pitched whine constantly. It's driving me crazy, especially at night. The filter is brand new.
image_description: Air purifier with all indicator lights green, filter indicator shows new, no visible damage or obstruction
text_context: Product: PureAir 500. SKU: PA-500. Warranty: 2 years. Coverage: Motor, fan, sensors, electrical. Common issues: Whining noise with new filter = motor bearing defect (not filter-related). High-pitched sound: Indicates motor resonance, not user-serviceable. Filter status: Green light confirms new filter properly seated.
ground_truth_reason: High-pitched whine with new filter and proper indicator lights = motor bearing defect. Image confirms filter seated (green light). Motor defect is covered and requires unit replacement, not repair.
